Stuffy epigastric sensation, fullness even with small intake, heaviness (head, body), loose stools, greasy coating → damp in SP (summer-damp invasion). Dislike greasy food, jaundice, hypochondriac pain, low degree fever → LV/GB damp-heat. Dislike to eat, belching with sour taste, abdominal distention with pain, thick-rotten coating → food stagnation. Pregnancy → dislike to eat, nausea, vomiting, slippery-rapid pulse

items.t: 3) Strong appetite with quick hunger (多食易飢)
items.detail: Like to eat, eat a lot, hungry again soon after eating. Strong appetite, easily hungry + thirst, agitation, red tongue with yellow coating, bad breath, constipation → ST fire. Strong appetite, easily hungry + loose stools → SP/ST disharmony (strong ST constraining SP, 脾約證)

items.t: 4) Hungry but no appetite (飢不欲食)
items.detail: Feel hungry but no desire to eat. Chronic uncomfortable (burning) sensation in ST, red tongue with scanty coating, thin-rapid pulse → insufficiency of ST Yin

items.t: 5) Others
items.detail: Parasite accumulation in small children. Pregnancy → like to eat sour food, no menses, nausea. Deserted middle (除中) → sudden increase of appetite in a severe condition, emergency

items.t: Having a special or strange taste in the mouth
No sense of taste (bland, 口淡乏味) + poor appetite → SP/ST Qi deficiency, (HT)
Sweet or greasy taste in the mouth → SP/ST damp-heat
Abundant sour taste → accumulation of heat in LV/ST
Sour and rotten taste → food stagnation
Bitter taste → Heat (LV/GB heat, HT fire)
Salty taste → KI, cold
Sweet for SP, sour for LV, etc. → deficiency of the corresponding Zangfu organ

blocks.heading: Thirst (渴症)
blocks.lead: Insufficient body fluids or blockage of fluids distribution → thirst

items.t: No thirst (口不渴)
items.detail: No damage to the body fluids → normal, cold, no prominent heat evil yet

items.t: Thirst with drinking a lot (口渴多飮) — severe damage of body fluids
items.detail: 1) Excess heat (實熱): great thirst with preference for cold water + red face, fever, restlessness, agitation, profuse sweating, large-surging pulse — interior excess heat severely damages body fluids, drinking is self-treatment. 2) Xiao Ke (wasting and thirst, 消渴): great thirst with drinking a lot + hunger with eating a lot, profuse urination, emaciation — LU heat (thirst) + ST heat (eating) + KI Yin deficiency (urination). 3) Temporary damage of body fluids: usually after profuse sweating, severe vomiting, or diarrhea

items.t: Thirst but little desire to drink (渴不多飮)
items.detail: Dry mouth or sensation of thirst + no desire to drink or drink just a little. Light body fluids damage, or problems with distribution of body fluids: Yin deficiency, damp-heat, phlegm-fluids, blood stasis

items.t: 1) Yin deficiency
items.detail: Dry mouth with little desire to drink + tidal fever, night sweating, red cheeks. Insufficient body fluids unable to nourish the mouth → dry mouth + no desire to drink (no excessive heat damaging body fluids)

items.t: 2) Damp-heat
items.detail: Thirst with desire to drink a little + heaviness (head, body), low degree fever, stuffy epigastric sensation, greasy coating. Damp-heat hinders transformation of body fluids → fluids unable to nourish the mouth, but not severe thirst because of internal damp

items.t: 3) Phlegm-fluids
items.detail: Thirst with preference for warm water but just a little, sometimes vomiting upon drinking + dizziness, borborygmus. Phlegm-fluids (Yin pathogen) damage Yang → unable to transform body fluids and nourish the mouth → thirst but no desire to drink because of internal damp

items.t: 4) Blood stasis
items.detail: Dry mouth, only rinses the mouth with water without swallowing + uneven pulse, stasis spots on the tongue. Blood stasis blockage impairs Qi transformation of body fluids → dry mouth + no damage to body fluids (no drinking)

items.t: "[When] Yang declines and Yin becomes exuberant, the eyes close. [When] Yin declines and Yang becomes exuberant, [one] wakes up."
items.cn: 陽氣盡, 陰氣盛, 則目瞑; 陰氣盡而陽氣盛, 則寤矣
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 28 (靈樞: 口問)>

items.t: Under normal condition, when Wei Qi is at the Yang channels, Yang is replete and one is awake; when Wei Qi moves into Yin channels at night, Yin becomes replete and one sleeps
When Yin and Yang lose harmony — Yang not entering Yin at night, or Yang unable to come out from Yin during the day — sleep problems (insomnia or lethargy) may occur

blocks.heading: Insomnia (失眠, 不寐)
blocks.lead: Relative imbalance between Yin and Yang (replete Yang, deficient Yin) → Yang unable to enter Yin → no place for spirit to rest → insomnia

items.t: 1) HT and KI disharmony (心腎不交)
items.detail: Difficulty falling asleep + agitation, dream a lot, tidal fever, night sweating, weak/sore lower back and knees. KI Yin deficiency or HT fire → KI water and HT fire unable to control each other → restless spirit

items.t: 2) HT and SP deficiency (心脾兩虛)
items.detail: Waking up often after sleep + palpitation, poor appetite, weakness, pale tongue. SP deficiency → unable to transform/transport food essence → HT blood deficiency → unable to nourish the spirit

items.t: 3) GB stagnation and phlegm harassing (膽鬱痰擾)
items.detail: Insomnia + easily frightened, dizziness, stuffy chest, agitation, bitter taste, nausea. Emotional stress → Qi stagnation → turns into fire and produces phlegm → phlegm-fire disturbing the spirit

items.t: 4) Food stagnation
items.detail: Stuffy epigastric sensation, belching, abdominal distention, thick-greasy tongue coating. Improper diet (overeating) damages SP/ST → ST Qi unable to properly descend + turbid Qi rising → disturbing the spirit

blocks.heading: Lethargy (嗜睡, 多眠)
blocks.lead: Tiredness, likes to sleep

items.t: 1) Phlegm-damp in SP (痰濕困脾)
items.detail: Tired and falls asleep easily + dizziness, heavy sensation, greasy tongue coating. External summer-damp invasion or internal build-up of phlegm-damp disturbs SP → clear Yang unable to ascend → unable to nourish the head

items.t: 2) SP Qi deficiency (脾氣虛弱)
items.detail: Tired and sleepy after meals + weakness, poor appetite, pale tongue. SP/ST Qi deficiency → weakened transforming/transporting → clear Yang unable to ascend → unable to nourish the head

items.t: 3) HT and KI Yang deficiency (心腎陽衰)
items.detail: Tired and sleepy, cold limbs, weak pulse. HT/KI Yang deficiency + exuberant Yin cold → declined normal bodily function

items.t: 4) Febrile disease entering the Ying blood level (溫病熱入心包)
items.detail: Unclear mind and sleepy, delirious speech, fever, skin lesions, deep-red tongue, rapid pulse. Warm febrile disease invasion penetrating into PC → exuberant heat misting the spirit

items.t: Lower 2 orifices are the body's gates where cold-heat and excess-deficiency may be observed
Observe frequency, amount, color, smell, and other qualities
Organs involved
items.detail: Intestines (LI, SI) — actual pathway; SP/ST — rotting, transforming, ascending, descending; LV — smooth flow of Qi; KI (Mingmen, 命門) — providing heat. [Note] LU — interior-exterior relationship with LI

items.t: Normal bowel movement
items.detail: Regular frequency: 1/day or every other day + emptied sensation. Moist: neither dry nor loose, forms. No blood, sticky discharge, or undigested food

items.t: General approaches
items.detail: Deficiency → condition aggravates after bowel movement. Excess → condition alleviates after bowel movement. Heat → strong foul smell. Cold → no smell or fish smell

items.t: Prolonged intervals between bowel movements, or inability to push out stools even with urge
Excess heat (熱秘)
items.detail: Fever, abdominal distention and pain, red tongue with yellow-dry coating. Heat damages body fluids → dry intestines and stools

items.t: Excess cold (冷秘)
items.detail: Pale-white complexion, preference for heat, deep-slow pulse. Excess cold blocks the intestines' movement

items.t: Yin deficiency
items.detail: Dry stools, red tongue with scanty coating, thin-rapid pulse. Insufficient Yin fluids unable to nourish the intestines

items.t: Qi and/or body fluids deficiency
items.detail: Old age, post-partum — weakness to push out + dryness. Not enough Qi to push out stools + body fluids/blood unable to nourish the intestines

blocks.heading: Diarrhea (泄瀉)
blocks.lead: Loose, unformed, or watery stools with increased frequency

items.t: SP deficiency
items.detail: Poor appetite, dull large-abdominal pain, loose stools. SP deficiency → unable to properly transform/transport → improper separating of clear and turbid (by SP & SI) → excessive fluids retained in the intestine

items.t: KI Yang deficiency
items.detail: Chronic early morning (5am) abdominal pain followed by diarrhea (pain relieved after), sore lower back and knees. Declining Ming Men fire (命門火) unable to warm SP (becoming cold) → SP unable to transform/transport + struggle between Yin and Yang (early morning = Yang beginning to rise)

items.t: LV Qi stagnation over-controlling SP
items.detail: Emotional stress triggering abdominal pain and diarrhea, pain reduced/relieved after diarrhea

items.t: Food stagnation
items.detail: Stuffy epigastric sensation, belching with foul smell, abdominal pain relieved by diarrhea (eliminating turbid Qi). Improper diet (overeating, wrong food) damages ST and intestines

items.t: Damp
items.detail: Diarrhea with mucus

items.t: SP and/or KI deficiency
items.detail: Undigested food, incontinence of diarrhea

items.t: Others (quality of stools, sensation during bowel movement)
items.detail: Sometimes dry, sometimes loose stools → LV and SP disharmony (LV Qi stagnation over-controlling SP): abdominal distention, alternating diarrhea and constipation, difficulty having bowel movement although stools not dry. Dry stools first, loose afterward → SP deficiency. Unfinished sensation → LV Qi stagnation over-controlling SP (stagnation of intestinal Qi). Undigested food in stools → SP and/or KI deficiency

items.t: Bleeding
items.detail: Bleeding before stools (near-site) + bright color → heat. Bleeding after stools (distal-site) + dark color (dark chocolate) → SP Qi unable to hold blood

items.t: Burning sensation at the anus during bowel movement + foul smell → damp-heat
Incontinence → SP/KI Yang deficiency
Urgency of bowel movement and heavy sensation afterwards → damp-heat
Prolapsed rectum → SP Qi deficiency and sinking
Organs involved
items.detail: BL, SJ — pathway. KI — Qi transformation + distribution of body fluids. LU, SP — distribution of body fluids

items.t: Normal
items.detail: 3–5 times during the day and 0–1 during the night. Good flow + emptied sensation. No abnormal sensation (pain, burning, etc.). No blood, protein, etc.

items.t: General approaches
items.detail: Cold → pale-clear color + profuse urination. Heat → dark color + scanty urination

items.t: Profuse urination
items.detail: Profuse + pale (clear) color, aversion to cold → empty-cold. Profuse, thirst, hungry, emaciation → Xiao Ke (消渴) [LU, ST, KI]

items.t: Scanty urination
items.detail: Scanty + dark color → excess heat (damaging body fluids). Scanty + swelling of the body → edema (LU/SP/KI malfunction)

items.t: Frequent urination
items.detail: Frequent, clear urine, urinary incontinence, night urination (enuresis), large amount or dribbling, usually in the elderly → KI deficiency (KI unable to control the lower orifice)

items.t: Incontinence
items.detail: Unable to hold + clear mind → unsecure KI Qi + BL unable to control urine. Enuresis → KI Qi deficiency + weak BL

items.t: Lin syndrome (淋證)
items.detail: Frequent urination, scanty-dark urine, urinary urgency, painful and/or burning urination. Damp-heat accumulation in the lower Jiao → inhibited Qi transformation function

items.t: Long Bi (difficult urination, 癃閉)
items.detail: Difficult (Long, 癃) or blocked (Bi, 閉) urination. Damp-heat, blood stasis, stone, KI Yang deficiency, Qi deficiency

blocks.heading: Women (婦女) — Menses (月經)
blocks.lead: Normal cycle: onset 14 (13) years → menopause 49 (48) years; cycle ~28 days; duration 5–7 days; proper red color without clots

items.t: 1) Early menses (月經先期)
items.detail: Menses coming about 1 week or more earlier than usual. Sticky dark-red menstrual blood + large amount → blood-heat (heat causing reckless movement of blood). Watery pale-red menstrual blood + large amount → Qi deficiency (Qi unable to hold blood)

items.t: 2) Late menses (月經後期)
items.detail: Menses coming about 1 week or more later than usual. Watery pale-red menstrual blood, scanty amount → blood deficiency. Dark menstrual blood with clots, scanty amount → cold (blood stasis)

items.t: 3) Irregular menses (月經前後不定期)
items.detail: Menses coming about 1 week or more early or late than usual. Relatively dark-red blood with clots, scanty amount, distended/painful breast → Qi stagnation. Watery pale-red blood with clots, scanty (KI) or large (SP) amount → SP/KI deficiency

items.t: 4) Painful menses / dysmenorrhea (痛經)
items.detail: Lower abdominal pain before, during, or after menses. Severe pain before onset, relieved afterward → excess (Qi stagnation, blood stasis) — pain due to blockage (不通則痛). Dull pain after menses, sore lower back → deficiency (Qi and blood, KI) — dull pain due to improper nourishment. Cold pain during menses, alleviated by warmth → cold (cold blocking the channels → contraction of uterus and collaterals → acute pain)

items.t: 5) No menses / amenorrhea
items.detail: No menses for 3+ consecutive months (except menopause, pregnancy, or breast feeding) → blood stasis, LV Qi stagnation, Xu Lao (虛勞)

items.t: 6) Beng Lou (abnormal bleeding: metrorrhagia or menorrhagia, 崩漏)
items.detail: Heat → dark blood with clots. Deficiency → pale-red blood without clots

items.t: Leukorrhea (帶下)
items.detail: Normal: scanty, clear (transparent), no smell. White, large amount, watery, no smell → white leukorrhea (白帶), cold-damp. Yellowish, large amount, sticky, foul smell → yellow leukorrhea (黃帶), damp-heat. Blood-tainted or mixed red-white, sticky, some smell → LV fire (LV Qi stagnation turning into fire). Red-profuse leukorrhea after menopause → cancer

items.t: Pregnancy (妊娠)
items.detail: Infertility → deficiency (blood, KI), excess (blood stasis, damp-heat). Miscarriage → deficiency (blood, KI, SP), excess (blood stasis). Vomiting (妊娠惡阻) → ST, LV

items.t: Postpartum (産後) — Lochia (bloody discharge, 産後惡露)
items.detail: Qi deficiency + sinking → large amount, pale color, fatigue, pale complexion. Blood heat → dark-red sticky, thirst, red complexion, constipation. Blood stasis → purplish-dark with clots, low abdominal pain that dislikes pressure

items.t: Postpartum fever (産後發熱)
items.detail: External wind invasion → fever and chills, body ache, headache. Exuberant fire evil → high fever, restlessness, thirst, dry mouth, constipation, dark urine. Blood deficiency + heat → low degree fever, dull abdominal pain, dizziness, pale complexion, dry stools

items.t: Postpartum depression
items.detail: Usually due to blood deficiency

blocks.heading: Children (小兒)
blocks.lead: Same general questions apply, with additional age-specific considerations

items.t: 1) Newborn / neonate (up to 28 days after delivery, 新生兒)
items.detail: Pregnancy: general health or diet of the mother, emotional stress or physical trauma, alcohol/smoking/drugs during pregnancy, ... Delivery: premature birth (<37 weeks), difficult labor, Cesarean, ...

items.t: 2) Infant (1 month–1/2 years) and toddler (1/2–3 years)
items.detail: Breast feeding and weaning (accustoming to food other than mother's milk). Weaning too early → retention of food, skin diseases

items.t: 3) Children
items.detail: Digestive (poor appetite, diarrhea, constipation), respiratory (whooping cough, high fever), and/or sleep problems. Vaccinations

blocks.heading: Palpating Diagnostics (切診) — Pulse Diagnosis (脈診)

items.t: Palpation of the body (按診, 觸診)
items.detail: skin, chest, abdomen, limbs, acupuncture points, etc.

items.t: [Note] Pulse diagnosis classics
items.detail: <Pulse Classic (Mai Jing, 脈經)>, WANG Shu He (王叔和, 210–258 CE). <Lakeside Pulse Study (Bin Hu Mai Xue, 瀕湖脈學)>, LI Shi Zhen (李時珍, 1518–1593)

blocks.heading: Why do we do pulse diagnosis?
blocks.lead: Clinical significances of pulse — changes in pulse show:

items.t: Depth of disease → interior vs. exterior
items.detail: [Ex] floating or deep pulse

items.t: Nature of evil Qi → heat vs. cold
items.detail: [Ex] rapid or slow pulse

items.t: State of right and evil Qi → excess vs. deficiency
items.detail: [Ex] weak or forceful pulse

items.t: Progress of a condition → recovering or worsening
items.detail: Chronic condition + pulse becoming moderate → recovery of ST Qi. Chronic deficiency condition + pulse becoming forceful-flooding → worsening, critical

blocks.heading: Relationship between Zangfu organs and the pulse (脈象形成原理)

items.t: HT governs blood and vessels (心主血脈)
items.detail: Circulation of blood within the blood vessels

items.t: LU controls channels and vessels (肺朝百脈)
items.detail: Distribution of blood to the entire body

items.t: SP governs holding of blood (脾主統血)
items.detail: Post-heaven (source of blood) + containing blood within the vessels

items.t: LV stores blood (肝藏血)
items.detail: Smooth flow and volume control of blood

items.t: KI stores essence (腎藏精)
items.detail: Pre-heaven (source of blood) + transforming Qi into blood

blocks.heading: Locations where the pulse is taken

items.t: Whole body approach
items.detail: Not commonly used by modern practitioners; sometimes used in emergency or when pulse is not detected from the radial artery. Includes the modern radial artery pulse (Cunkou Mai, 寸口脈)

items.t: Radial artery approach (寸口脈法)

blocks.heading: Whole body approach — 3 places and 9 states (三部九候脈法)
blocks.lead: Source: <Su Wen: Chapter 20 (素問: 三部九候論)>. Taking pulses from 3 places of the body (upper, middle, lower) x 3 levels each (heaven, human, earth) = 9 states

blocks.heading: Whole body approach — 3 places (三部脈法)
blocks.lead: Source: <Shang Han Lun (傷寒論)>. Taking pulse from carotid artery, instep, radial artery + foot Shaoyin pulse

blocks.heading: Radial artery pulse approach (寸口脈法)

items.t: Most commonly used in modern pulse diagnosis
Source
items.detail: <Nei Jing (內經)>, <Nan Jing (難經)>, <Mai Jing (脈經)>

items.t: [Note] Also called Cunkou pulse (寸口脈) or radial pulse
1) 3 positions (三部: 寸關尺)
items.detail: Middle (Guan, 關) — ulna side of the styloid process of the radius. Distal (Cun, 寸) — between the Guan position and the wrist. Proximal (Chi, 尺) — immediately proximal to the Guan position

blocks.heading: 3 positions (Cun/Guan/Chi) and corresponding Zangfu organs — simplified
blocks.lead: Different classical sources (Nan Jing, Mai Jing, Jing Yue Quan Shu, Golden Mirror of Medical Tradition, Bin Hu Mai Xue) give slightly different correspondences. Commonly used simplified version:

items.t: Left hand → HT (distal/Cun), LV (middle/Guan), KI (proximal/Chi)
Right hand → LU (distal/Cun), SP/ST (middle/Guan), KI (proximal/Chi)
Distal (Cun) → upper part of the body; Middle (Guan) → middle part of the body; Proximal (Chi) → lower part of the body

rows: Jing Yue Quan Shu (景岳全書)
KI 腎/BL 膀胱, LI 大腸
KI 腎/SJ 三焦, Mingmen 命門, SI 小腸
Golden Mirror of Medical Tradition (醫宗金鑑)
KI 腎/BL 膀胱, SI 小腸
Bin Hu Mai Xue (瀕湖脈學)

blocks.heading: 3 levels (depths, 浮中沉) of pulse and corresponding Zangfu organs
blocks.lead: Different pressure applied by the fingers = different levels; applies regardless of left or right

rows: State of Qi / Yang organs; Upper Jiao; Exterior conditions
State of blood; Middle Jiao; SP/ST disorders
State of Yin; Lower Jiao; Interior conditions

items.t: 1) Time
items.detail: Ideally, early in the morning when Yin is calm and Yang has not yet come forth

items.t: 2) Position
items.detail: Patient's arm: parallel (not held higher than HT level) + relaxed. Practitioner: sitting on the side of the patient. Use the left (or right) hand of the practitioner to palpate the right (or left) pulse of the patient. First place the middle finger on Guan (middle), then the index finger on Cun (distal), then the ring finger on Chi (proximal). Use the finger-print areas of the fingers to palpate — natural arching of fingers. [Note] Children — use only the practitioner's thumb (no need to divide 3 positions)

items.t: 3) Breathing of the practitioner
items.detail: One breathing (exhaling and inhaling) is the basis for determining slow or fast pulse. Focus on maintaining even and calm breathing

items.t: 4) Duration
items.detail: Palpate the pulse for a minimum of 50 beats; 100 (50x2), 150 (50x3) beats, etc. if more time is required

items.t: 5) 3 levels/depth (浮中沉: superficial, middle, and deep)
items.detail: Superficial (Fu, 浮): resting the fingers on the radial artery very gently. Middle (Zhong, 中): between superficial and deep. Deep (Chen, 沉): pressing hard to the bottom

blocks.heading: Normal pulse (平脈)
blocks.lead: Should have 3 qualities: ST Qi, spirit, and root — i.e. Qi, mind, and essence

items.t: 1) Speed
items.detail: 4 to 5 beats per breath = 72–80 beats/min

items.t: 2) ST Qi (胃氣)
items.detail: ST is the root of post-heaven, source of Qi and blood — presence or absence of ST Qi determines life or death. "Evil Qi coming is tight and rapid. Food Qi coming is moderate and harmonious" (邪氣來也緊而疾, 穀氣來也徐而和) <Ling Shu: Chapter 9 (靈樞: 終始)>. Moderate and harmonious pulse indicates the presence of ST Qi → health, good prognosis or favorable development even under illness

items.t: 3) Spirit (神)
items.detail: HT governs blood and vessels and stores the spirit — when Qi and blood are replete, the spirit becomes strong and the pulse has strength. Soft (but with strength) pulse indicates spirit — neither weak nor tight

items.t: 4) Root (根)
items.detail: KI is the root of the pre-heaven, source of the primordial force of the body. The pulse with root can be felt clearly at the deep level and proximal (Chi) position

items.t: 5) Natural changes to the normal pulse
items.detail: Seasons: more floating than normal during summer, deeper than normal during winter. Gender: men's pulse relatively stronger than women's; relatively stronger left-side (Yang) pulse for men and right-side (Yin) pulse for women; slippery-rapid pulse for pregnancy. Age: faster pulse for small children; stronger pulse for young people; weaker pulse for elderly. Body build: more floating pulse for thin people, deeper pulse for obese people. May also change due to short-term influences (running, emotional upset, little sleep with a long day's work, etc.)

blocks.heading: 28 Pulses (二十八脈) — cross-reference of English translations
When palpating the pulse, try to:

items.t: Feel the pulse as a whole
Look for the ST Qi, spirit, and root
Feel the strength of the pulse
Feel the different levels (3-level: superficial, middle, deep)
Feel the different positions (3-position: proximal, middle, distal)
Feel the quality of the pulse

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (1) — Floating, Deep, Slow, Rapid

items.t: Floating pulse (Fu Mai, 浮脈)
items.detail: Felt with light pressure, just resting fingers on the artery. Exterior pattern (表證): floating and slightly reduced going into deeper level — external evil invasion + right Qi moving to the surface to fight. Deficiency pattern (虛證): floating-large-forceless and empty at the deep level — deficient Qi floating outwards

items.t: Deep pulse (Chen Mai, 沉脈)
items.detail: Felt only with heavy pressure. Interior excess pattern (裏實證): deep + strong — stagnant evil Qi in the interior + right Qi struggling against it. Interior deficiency pattern (裏虛證): deep + weak — weak right Qi due to deficient Zangfu organs, unable to reach outwards

items.t: Slow pulse (Chi Mai, 遲脈)
items.detail: Less than 4 beats per breath (usually <60/min). Excess cold pattern (寒實證, 寒積證): slow-strong — cold evil making right Qi stagnant, difficulty moving. Empty cold pattern (虛寒證): slow-weak — Yang deficiency, weak right Qi. [Note] Excess heat accumulation (邪熱結聚): slow-strong — stagnation of evil heat causing sluggish movement of Qi and blood; Yangming pattern (陽明病) of purging type

items.t: Rapid pulse (Shuo Mai, 數脈)
items.detail: More than 5 beats per breath (usually >90/min). Excess heat pattern (實熱證): rapid-strong — exuberant heat evil accelerating movement of Qi and blood. Empty heat pattern (虛熱證): rapid-weak — Yin deficiency, empty heat. [Note] Yang deficiency floating outwards (陽虛外浮): rapid-floating-large-weak + empty at deep level — deficient Yang separating from Yin, floating alone outward/upward

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (2) — Full, Large, Thready, Feeble, Scatter

items.t: Full pulse (Hong Mai, 洪脈)
items.detail: Floating-large-rapid (coming forcefully but going away weak). Extreme heat (熱盛) → congested vessels with Qi and blood. Excess → heat in Qi level. Deficiency → exuberant evil Qi + weak right Qi

items.t: Large pulse (Da Mai, 大脈)
items.detail: Floating-large-empty, similar to Hong Mai but weak & empty (only felt at the edges). Deficiency, strong evil Qi with worsening condition

items.t: Thready pulse (Xi Mai, 細脈)
items.detail: Thin like a fine line, but felt clearly. Deficiency (blood, Qi), damp, Xu Lao (虛勞). Blood and/or Qi deficiency → unable to fill the vessels. Damp → blocks and presses the vessels. [Note] aka. Xiao Mai (small, 小脈)

items.t: Feeble pulse (Wei Mai, 微脈)
items.detail: Extremely thin (thinner than Xi Mai) and soft, difficult to feel, (deep) — too weak to move Qi and blood. Severe deficiency (Yang, Qi, Yin, blood). In chronic condition → extreme deficiency of right Qi. In acute condition → abrupt exhaustion of Yang Qi

items.t: Scatter pulse (San Mai, 散脈)
items.detail: Floating-scattered-large without root. Separating original Qi (critical condition). Floating-dispersed on light pressure, but no pulse on middle to deep pressure

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (3) — Long, Short, Slippery, Choppy, Moderate

items.t: Long pulse (Chang Mai, 長脈)
items.detail: Pulse extending beyond the normal pulse position. Heat (excess Yang), excessive LV Yang. [Note] Healthy if long-moderate — sufficient Qi and blood

items.t: Short pulse (Duan Mai, 短脈)
items.detail: Pulse occupying a shorter space than the normal pulse position; opposite of Chang Mai. Stagnation, deficiency. Short-forceless → Qi deficiency. Short-forceful → Qi (and blood) stagnation, (phlegm stagnation, food accumulation)

items.t: Slippery pulse (Hua Mai, 滑脈)
items.detail: Smooth-round, as if beads or pearls are rolling through. Phlegm-fluids, food stagnation, excess heat, pregnancy. [Note] Pregnant women with slippery-rapid pulse → sufficient Qi and blood, healthy. [Note] Slippery-moderate-harmonious → healthy person with sufficient Qi and blood

items.t: Choppy pulse (Se Mai, 澀脈)
items.detail: Rough-uneven without smooth quality; opposite of Hua Mai (changes in speed and quality: thin, slow, short, scattered). Deficiency (blood, body fluids, essence), Qi and blood stagnation. Choppy-forceless → deficiency, unable to fill the vessels. Choppy-forceful → Qi and blood stagnation, blockage causing unsmooth flow

items.t: Moderate pulse (Huan Mai, 緩脈)
items.detail: 4 beats per breath, coming and going moderately. Health, weak SP/ST, damp. Weak SP/ST + damp → moderate and lazy feeling. Health → moderate and harmonious

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (4) — Tight, Wiry, Deficient, Excessive

items.t: Tight pulse (Jin Mai, 緊脈)
items.detail: Tight and twisted, rope-like pulse. Excess cold, pain, retention of food. Cold evil blocking flow of Yang Qi → struggle between cold evil and right Qi → tight. Floating-tight → wind-cold. Deep-tight → interior cold

items.t: Wiry pulse (Xuan Mai, 弦脈)
items.detail: Tight like a guitar string — tighter and harder (thinner) than Jin Mai, not obvious on light pressure but tighter on middle/deep pressure. LV/GB problems, pain, phlegm-fluids, (malaria). Evil Qi affecting smooth flow of LV Qi → tensed Qi, pain, phlegm accumulation → wiry. Wiry-thin → LV overacting on SP/ST deficiency

items.t: Deficient pulse (Xu Mai, 虛脈)
items.detail: Soft-large (slow) + weak on pressure. Deficiency (Qi and blood, Zangfu organs)

items.t: Excessive pulse (Shi Mai, 實脈)
items.detail: Large-long-forceful on all positions and levels. Excess

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (5) — Hollow, Drum-skin, Firm, Weak, Soggy, Hidden

items.t: Hollow pulse (Kou Mai, 芤脈)
items.detail: Floating-soft + empty in the middle (like a scallion stalk). Loss of blood (hemorrhage), damaged Yin. Sudden loss of blood or severe body fluids damage → unable to fill up the vessels + Yang with no place to attach, scattered outwards

items.t: Drum-skin pulse (Ge Mai, 革脈)
items.detail: Floating-tight (like a stretched drum) at superficial level + empty on pressure. Loss of blood or essence, abortion (半産), vaginal bleeding (漏下)

items.t: Firm pulse (Lao Mai, 牢脈)
items.detail: Felt only at deep level + forceful-wiry. Interior cold, Shan Qi (hernia, 疝氣), abdominal masses (癥瘕)

items.t: Weak pulse (Ruo Mai, 弱脈)
items.detail: Only felt at deep level + soft-thin. Deficiency of Qi and blood

items.t: Soggy pulse (Ru Mai, 濡脈)
items.detail: Floating-thin-weak-soft + disappearing if pressured. Deficiency (blood, Yin), damp

items.t: Hidden pulse (Fu Mai, 伏脈)
items.detail: Only felt at bone level (extremely deep). Hidden evil in the deep-level Qi (邪閉), reversal syndrome (厥證), extreme pain (痛極). Hidden evil Qi → Qi cannot flow

blocks.heading: Individual pulses (6) — Moving, Skipping, Knotted, Intermittent, Racing

items.t: Moving pulse (Dong Mai, 動脈)
items.detail: Round (like a bean) + trembling + slippery-rapid-forceful. Pain, shock, fright. Usually seen in people severely traumatized by emotional shock or in severe pain

items.t: Skipping pulse (Cu Mai, 促脈)
items.detail: Rapid + missing beat (stopping) at irregular interval. Excess heat. Hasty-forceful → heat from Qi and blood stagnation, phlegm-fluids stagnation, or retention of food, (abscess). Hasty-forceless → critical

items.t: Knotted pulse (Jie Mai, 結脈)
items.detail: Slow + missing beat (stopping) at irregular interval. Cold, cold-phlegm, blood stasis, abdominal masses, (HT deficiency)

items.t: Intermittent pulse (Dai Mai, 代脈)
items.detail: Missing beat (stopping) at regular interval. Decline of Zang organs' Qi, wind, pain, fright, traumatic injuries. [Note] Stopping every 4 beats or less → serious

items.t: Racing pulse (Ji Mai, 疾脈)
items.detail: Extremely rapid-urgent (7–8 beats per breath). Extreme Yang with exhausted Yin. [Note] Small children may have this rapid pulse without any problems

items.t: Floating quality → floating, hollow, leather
Deep quality → deep, firm, hidden
Slow speed → slow, knotted
Rapid speed → rapid, hasty, hurried, moving
Empty quality → empty, weak, fine, minute, scattered, short
Full quality → full, overflowing, wiry, tight, long
1) Menses coming
items.detail: Relatively surging pulse on the left middle and distal positions + no other symptoms (bitter taste, fever, abdominal distention, etc.)

items.t: 2) No menses — amenorrhea
items.detail: General amenorrhea pulse → choppy at the Chi position. Blood deficiency if empty-thin-choppy pulse at Chi. Excess if wiry-choppy pulse at Chi

items.t: Pregnancy
items.detail: General pregnancy pulse → slippery-rapid with force and harmony

items.t: 3) Abortion
items.detail: Surging-choppy pulse → danger of losing the baby or already dead fetus

items.t: Use the thumb (at the middle position) to palpate the 3 levels
Age and normal beats per breath
items.detail: 0–3 years old: 7–8 beats/breath. 5–6 years old: 6 beats/breath (7/breath = rapid; ≤5/breath = slow)

items.t: Not fully developed KI Qi → normal to see no pulse at deep level

blocks.heading: Combined pulses and patterns
blocks.lead: Common to see a combination of different pulses — seldom to see only one pulse quality

items.t: Floating-tight (浮緊) → exterior wind-cold, wind-cold Bi
Floating-moderate (浮緩) → Taiyang wind-strike (exterior wind-cold deficiency)
Floating-rapid (浮數) → exterior wind-heat
Floating-slippery (浮滑) → exterior + phlegm (person with phlegm having exterior syndrome), wind-phlegm
Deep-slow (沉遲) → exterior cold (excess, deficiency)
Deep-wiry (沉弦) → LV Qi stagnation, water-fluids retention
Deep-choppy (沉澀) → blood stasis (Yang deficiency, cold)
Deep-moderate (沉緩) → SP deficiency + water-damp retention
Deep-thin (沉細) → deficiency (Yin, blood)
Wiry-rapid (弦數) → LV Qi stagnation turning into fire, LV/GB damp-heat
Wiry-thin (弦細) → LV & KI Yin deficiency, blood deficiency + LV Qi stagnation, LV Qi stagnation + SP deficiency
Slippery-rapid (滑數) → phlegm-fire, food stagnation + heat
Wiry-slippery-rapid (弦滑數) → LV fire + phlegm, LV Yang rising, retention of phlegm-fire
Surging-rapid (洪數) → excess heat at Qi level (external evil Qi attack)

blocks.heading: Pulse and correspondence of conditions
blocks.lead: Condition + expected pulse(s) = corresponding (順); condition + non-corresponding pulse(s) = against (逆)

items.t: Exterior pattern
items.detail: Corresponding (順): surging-rapid-excess (洪數實) — excess evil Qi + strong right Qi struggling. Against (逆): thin-weak (細弱) — excess evil Qi + weak right Qi, danger of evil Qi falling into the interior relatively easily

items.t: Acute condition
items.detail: Corresponding (順): floating (浮), surging (洪), rapid (數), excess (實) — strong right Qi fighting evil Qi. Against (逆): deep (沉), minute (微), thin (細), weak (弱) — already declined right Qi

items.t: Chronic condition
items.detail: Corresponding (順): deep (沉), minute (微), thin (細), weak (弱) — weak evil Qi + recovering right Qi. Against (逆): floating (浮), surging (洪), rapid (數), excess (實) — weak right Qi + strong evil Qi

items.t: Interpretation
items.detail: Not-corresponding pulse and condition = false pulse or condition + right pulse or condition. Right condition, false pulse — e.g. congested interior heat → reversal cold of the limbs + slippery-rapid pulse. Right pulse, false condition — e.g. excess heat in ST/intestines → distended painful abdomen dislikes pressure, dry constipated stools, red tongue with thick-yellow-dry coating + deep-slow pulse. Use all 4 diagnostics to make proper pattern differentiation

blocks.heading: Palpation of the Body (按診, 觸診)

items.t: 3 ways of palpating
items.detail: Touching (觸): using fingertips or palms, lightly touch local areas to check hot, cold, dryness, moist, etc. Seeking (摸): on the area of local swelling or distension, look for shape and size. Pressing (按): press local areas (usually chest or abdomen) to check pain on pressure, masses (size, quality), swelling, distension, etc.

blocks.heading: Palpating the skin
blocks.lead: In general, hot if Yang Qi is excessive and cold if Yang Qi is declined

items.t: 1) Interior and exterior
items.detail: Very hot at first touch but becoming less hot after a while → heat at the exterior. Becoming hotter with time → heat at the interior. Painful on light pressure → shallow level (exterior). Painful on relatively heavy pressure → deep level (interior)

items.t: 2) Excess and deficiency
items.detail: Soft skin + prefers pressure → deficiency. Affected site hard and dislikes pressure → excess

items.t: 3) Dry and moist
items.detail: Dry skin → tendency of no sweating. Dry and thinning → insufficiency of body fluids. Moist-wet → tendency to sweat easily. Dry and scaling → damaged Yin, dry blood

items.t: 4) Edema (swelling)
items.detail: Dips on pressure but rises back up quickly after removing pressure → Qi swelling (氣腫). Dips on pressure & unable to rise up quickly → water swelling (水腫)

items.t: 5) Skin lesions
items.detail: Swollen + hard + not hot → cold. Swollen + hot on touch + painful on pressure → heat. Depressed under the skin line → deficiency. Erupted above the skin line → excess. Hard and tight → no abscess yet. Hard surroundings and soft tip (center, top) → abscess formed

items.t: 1) General approach
items.detail: Cold limbs → cold (Yang deficiency, excess cold). Hot limbs → heat (excess heat, Yin deficiency)

items.t: 2) Others
items.detail: Stagnant heat in the interior, Qi stagnation (excess, heat) → reversal cold of the limbs. Relatively hotter dorsal sides of hands/feet → heat from exterior invasion. Relatively hotter palm/sole sides → interior heat. Relatively hotter forehead than palms → exterior heat. Relatively hotter palms than forehead → interior heat

items.t: 3) Small children
items.detail: Cold finger tips → fright reversal (hands cold due to fear). Only middle finger hot → wind-cold. Only middle finger tip cold → pox (chicken pox, smallpox) to occur

items.t: 4) Prognosis based on cold or warm limbs
items.detail: Yang deficiency with chills and diarrhea + warm limbs → treatable. Yang deficiency with chills and diarrhea + cold limbs → difficult to treat

items.t: 1) Palpating Xuli (虛裏)
items.detail: Normal: not tight or hasty, but moderate. Minute and indistinct → deficiency of Zong Qi (gathering/chest Qi, 宗氣). Surging-large-bouncing → emergency

items.t: 2) Palpating the chest and hypochondriac area
items.detail: Distended chest and panting on pressure → LU distention (肺脹). Distending pain on pressing the chest/hypochondriac area → phlegm-heat stagnation, water-fluids retention. Enlarged-swollen LV + soft or hard → Qi stagnation, blood stasis

items.t: 3) Palpating the abdomen — cold and hot
items.detail: Cold abdominal skin + likes pressure → empty-cold. Hot abdominal skin + dislikes pressure → excess heat

items.t: Pain (疼痛)
items.detail: Abdominal pain + likes pressure → deficiency. Abdominal pain + dislikes pressure → excess. Local burning sensation + intolerable pain → abscess

items.t: Distention and fullness (脹滿)
items.detail: Abdominal distension + hard & painful on pressure → excess type. Abdominal distension + not hard & no pain on pressure → deficiency type

items.t: Piman (stuffy and fullness sensation at the epigastric area, 痞滿)
items.detail: Soft and no pain on pressure → deficiency. Relatively hard and painful on pressure → excess. Distended + pain on pressure + water sound → water-fluids retention in ST

items.t: Jiexiong (knotted chest, 結胸)
items.detail: Stuffy and distended epigastric area + pain on pressure → small Jiexiong (小結胸). Hard and full epigastric area + pain (dislikes pressure) → large Jiexiong (大結胸)

items.t: Masses (腫塊) — accumulation and gathering (Ji Ju, 積聚)
items.detail: Pain + fixed location + shape (boundaries) → accumulation (積), blood level (blood stasis). Pain + no fixed location + without shape → gathering (聚), Qi level (Qi stagnation). Left lesser abdominal pain + hard masses → stagnated stools. Right lesser abdominal pain + pain on pressure → intestinal abscess (腸癰). Accumulation of parasites → feels like sinews, moving, erupted or depressed skin

items.t: Look for knots or other congested sensation, pain, or sensitive reaction
Examples
items.detail: LU disorders → knots on BL13, painful LU1. ST disorders → BL21 and/or ST36 pain. Intestinal abscess (腸癰) → painful ST37 (闌尾穴)
